seo.title: LLM Jailbreak Prevention for AI Agents in Flowise Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix llm jailbreak prevention for ai agents in Flowise agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Flowise, LLM jailbreaks manifest when malicious user inputs bypass validation in chatflow chains, exploiting unfiltered intermediate outputs from nodes like LLMChain or AgentExecutor. Drag-and-drop configurations often lack built-in prompt guards, allowing attacks to propagate through sequential agent handoffs and custom tool nodes. This is particularly dangerous in multi-agent flows where context loss between handoffs amplifies jailbreak success rates.

howItManifests.scenario: Chatflow chains with unvalidated LLMChain outputs
howItManifests.symptom: User inputs 'ignore previous instructions' pass through without filtering
howItManifests.impact: Downstream agents execute malicious system prompts

howItManifests.scenario: Vector store retrieval in RAG flows
howItManifests.symptom: Jailbreak prompts retrieved as 'relevant' context documents
howItManifests.impact: LLM receives attack instructions as legitimate context

howItManifests.scenario: Custom tool nodes without input sanitization
howItManifests.symptom: Tools execute raw user payloads without validation
howItManifests.impact: Arbitrary code execution or data exfiltration via tools

howItManifests.symptom: Jailbreak tokens dropped during agent handoffs
howItManifests.impact: Second agent receives clean context but corrupted instructions

detection.name: Jailbreak Prompt Regex Scanner
detection.description: Scans chatflow input payloads for 50+ known jailbreak patterns before LLMChain execution

detection.name: Agent Handoff Context Auditor
detection.description: Monitors context transfer between sequential agents for jailbreak token injection

detection.name: Tool Input Payload Inspector
detection.description: Intercepts custom tool calls to validate parameters against allowlists

fixes.name: Input Sanitization Wrapper Node
fixes.description: Flowise custom node that wraps all LLMChain inputs with multi-layer filtering

fixes.name: Secure Agent Handoff Middleware
fixes.description: Validates context between multi-agent transitions with checksum verification

fixes.name: Allowlist Tool Executor
fixes.description: Replaces vulnerable custom tools with sandboxed, parameterized versions

vexAutomation.heading: Automating LLM Jailbreak Prevention for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a callback handler directly into Flowise chatflow configuration, automatically intercepting all node executions to detect jailbreak patterns in real-time. It monitors the entire execution graph - from initial user input through agent handoffs and tool calls - using Flowise's built-in callback system without requiring code changes to existing flows.

Vex employs runtime behavioral analysis, tracking anomalies like sudden instruction changes, encoded payloads in vector retrievals, and suspicious tool parameter patterns. When jailbreaks are detected, Vex can automatically reroute flows through remediation nodes, quarantine malicious sessions, and generate detailed attack reports with flow traces.

The platform provides continuous monitoring with zero-downtime deployment, automatically updating jailbreak pattern databases and adapting to new attack vectors through federated learning across all Flowise deployments.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add one line of Vex callback code to protect all your Flowise agents from jailbreaks instantly.
